provenance.note: The Nong Shu records a horizontal waterwheel as the source of the transmission.

radius.note: The 3.0 m radius is the chosen reconstruction value for the 5:1 cord-drive demonstration.

@rest.note: Wheel width and spoke count are inferred display dimensions.

provenance.note: The source describes a timber frame and upright shaft beside the rapid stream.

@rest.note: Shaft radius and rendered length are inferred from the exhibit layout.

provenance.note: The Nong Shu explicitly runs a cord from the upper horizontal wheel to the forward drum.

@rest.note: The two quarter-turn cord spans, section, and tangent offsets follow the inferred perpendicular wheel placement.

provenance.note: The source names the forward rotating drum driven by the cord.

radius.note: The 0.6 m radius is chosen with the 3.0 m waterwheel radius to demonstrate a 5:1 speed-up.

@rest.note: Drum width and spoke count are inferred display dimensions.

provenance.note: The source states that the crank branch turns with the forward drum.

@rest.note: The visible crank disc, width, and spoke count are inferred around the 0.24 m crank throw.

provenance.note: The source says the crank passes through the connecting beam and pushes and pulls it.

length.note: The 1.2 m connecting-rod length is an inferred reconstruction parameter.

@rest.note: Rod thickness is inferred for legibility and collision sampling.

provenance.note: The source names the front upright timber moved by the connecting beam and rocker.

joint.limits.0.note: Zero is the reconstructed closed-stroke datum.

joint.limits.1.note: The 0.5 m travel limit safely contains the inferred 0.48 m stroke.

@rest.note: Upright cross-section and 0.6 m rendered length are inferred to preserve clearance below the forward drum throughout the stroke.

provenance.note: The Nong Shu describes the paired rocker ears between the connecting beam and bellows timber.

@rest.note: Rocker dimensions, fixed transform carrier, and display placement are inferred; its geometry converts the solved linear state to the exact linkage angle.

provenance.note: The source records the bellows timber that reciprocates under the rocker transmission.

size.1.note: The data card preserves the scattered approximately three-chi timber length as 0.94 m.

joint.limits.0.note: Zero is the reconstructed closed-stroke datum.

joint.limits.1.note: The 0.5 m travel limit safely contains the inferred 0.48 m stroke.

@rest.note: Board width and thickness are inferred display dimensions.

provenance.note: The recorded apparatus is a water-powered bellows installation; this chest locates the reconstructed working board.

@rest.note: Chest dimensions and offset are inferred exhibit geometry rather than recorded measurements.
